brief_document.title: EngineBuilder and build()
brief_document.purpose: Provide the single assembly path for the engine. The builder collects a store, scheduler thread count, loaded workflows, and NIFs, then build() wires the runtime, installs NIFs and modules, repopulates the registry from the store, and returns a live Engine. This is the one construction path (D10).
brief_document.task: Implement engine/builder.rs: EngineBuilder with new(), store(...), scheduler_threads(n), load_workflows(...), register_nifs(...), and build(). build() constructs the RuntimeHandle from the thread count, installs the collected NIFs, loads the workflow packages (registering namespaced modules and recording versions), re-creates per-type supervisors and repopulates the registry from the store's list_active (delegating the actual replay to AD), and returns the Engine. A missing store is EngineError::MissingStore. Scope is the builder and build(); the Engine methods are AE-012.

requirements.title: Builder surface
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide EngineBuilder with new(), store(impl EventStore), scheduler_threads(n), load_workflows(...), register_nifs(...), and build(). THE SYSTEM SHALL accept the scheduler thread count from scheduler_threads and SHALL NOT default it to a hardcoded number.
acceptance: builder.rs defines EngineBuilder::new() and the chainable setters store, scheduler_threads, load_workflows, and register_nifs returning Self.
store accepts any impl EventStore + Send + Sync + 'static, stored as Arc<dyn EventStore>.
scheduler_threads(n) records Some(n); if never called, the builder holds None (passed through to beamr per AE-002).
load_workflows accepts package source(s) (a path and/or an already-loaded Package) and register_nifs accepts a host NIF set.
engine/mod.rs contains only pub mod and pub use.

requirements.title: build() assembles the runtime, NIFs, and modules
requirements.spec: WHEN build() is called, THE SYSTEM SHALL construct the RuntimeHandle from the configured thread count, install the engine's own and host-supplied NIFs before any workflow runs, and load the configured workflow packages (registering namespaced modules and recording versions). IF no store was supplied, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return EngineError::MissingStore.
acceptance: build() with no store returns EngineError::MissingStore.
build() constructs the RuntimeHandle (AE-002) with the configured Option<usize> thread count.
build() installs all NIFs (AE-003) before loading or starting anything; a duplicate MFA surfaces as EngineError::NifRegistration.
build() loads each configured package via the loader (AE-005); a load failure surfaces as EngineError::Load with nothing partially registered.
A test builds an Engine over InMemoryStore with one loaded fixture package and asserts success; a test omitting the store asserts MissingStore.

requirements.title: Repopulate registry and supervisors from list_active
requirements.spec: WHEN build() completes runtime setup, THE SYSTEM SHALL read the store's list_active, re-create the per-type supervisor for each active workflow's type, and repopulate the registry, delegating the actual state reconstruction (replay) to AD.
acceptance: build() calls EventStore::list_active and, for each active workflow, ensures its per-type supervisor (AE-006) and inserts a registry handle whose status is reconciled from its event history (AE-004 reconcile).
The actual replay of workflow state is delegated to AD via a documented seam (a hook/trait call), not implemented here.

A test seeds InMemoryStore with one active workflow's WorkflowStarted, builds the Engine, and asserts the registry contains a handle for it with the projected status and its per-type supervisor exists.
An empty store yields an Engine with an empty registry and no per-type supervisors.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the replay engine — build() triggers AD's replay via a seam; reconstruction is AD's.
SHALL NOT implement the Engine's runtime methods (start/cancel/result/list/shutdown) — those are AE-012.
SHALL NOT add networking or a server — build() wires only the embedded runtime, store, registry, and supervision.
verification: cargo test -p aion passes against InMemoryStore.
build() is the only assembly path and validates a store is present (D10).
No hardcoded scheduler thread default in aion (CO11).

checklist.text: EngineBuilder offers new(), store(impl EventStore), scheduler_threads(n), load_workflows(...), register_nifs(...), and build().

build() wires the runtime, registers NIFs and loaded modules, repopulates the registry and re-creates supervisors from the store's list_active (delegating replay to AD), and returns EngineError::MissingStore if no store was supplied.

stories.text: As an engine operator, I want to build an Engine from a store, loaded workflows, and my NIFs with one builder so that embedding durable workflows in my Rust app is a single, clear assembly step.
As an engine operator, I want to set the scheduler thread count (and have a sensible runtime default when I don't) so that I tune concurrency to my host without the engine guessing a number for me.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a runtime gate it at runtime, never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion depends only on aion-core, aion-store, aion-package, and beamr among workspace crates — no aion-server, no store backend crate, no networking crate. Structural; must hold.
Transport-agnostic: no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket dependency or code anywhere in the crate.
Only the runtime module imports beamr; every other module uses the RuntimeHandle (per D1).
All library errors are thiserror EngineError variants; no anyhow in this library crate. No .unwrap()/.expect() outside tests.
Mutex/RwLock poison handled explicitly and mapped to a typed EngineError; no .unwrap() on a lock guard.
WorkflowStatus is read from aion-core's event projection; the registry's cached status is never the source of truth (per aion-core CO7).
aion hardcodes no scheduler thread count, no timeout, and no retry default; configurable values come from the builder or are deferred to beamr's own default.
Module namespacing uses aion-package's transform unchanged; the engine never re-derives namespaced names (per D8).
Engine tests run against InMemoryStore; no test requires a database (per aion-core D8).

resolved_context.intention: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows, runs each as a BEAM process on an embedded beamr runtime, supervises them through a three-level tree, dispatches their activities as linked child processes, and cancels or reports on them by ID. Starting a workflow is spawning a process; cancelling it is killing that process, which through links kills its activity children too; an activity crash propagates an exit signal the trapping workflow process receives as a message. The crate is transport-agnostic — no HTTP/gRPC/WebSocket — keeping the embedded path free of network dependencies. This cluster owns lifecycle, process management, supervision, and module loading; it defers durability/replay (AD) and time/signals/queries/children/concurrency (AT) to their owners, surfacing their entry points on Engine and stating the seams explicitly.

provenance.quote: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows,
